judgment.summary: LULU shows strong historical profitability and returns but faces current headwinds in revenue and earnings growth, trading at a reasonable valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus; next earnings date is 2026-06-04.
positives: Gross margin 56.6% and operating margin 22.3% indicate strong pricing power and operational efficiency.
Return on equity 34.0% and return on assets 17.2% demonstrate effective use of shareholder and asset capital.
Debt/Equity at 36.24% and a current ratio of 2.26 suggest a healthy balance sheet with manageable leverage.
Free cash flow of $824.08M provides ample liquidity.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimate by 4.8%.
negatives: Revenue growth of 0.8% YoY and quarterly earnings growth of -21.6% indicate a slowdown.
Analyst estimates project further declines in current quarter (-35.0%) and current fiscal year (-7.2%) earnings.
Forward P/E of 9.34 is similar to trailing P/E, suggesting limited expected earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is 'hold' with a wide dispersion in price targets.

news.paragraph: A recent headline mentions a film's high AI compute costs, which is not directly related to LULU's core business of athletic apparel. This news item is likely noise and does not provide actionable insight for LULU traders. The upcoming earnings report on June 4th is the primary catalyst to watch.

headlines.title: This Cannes Film Cost $500,000 to Make. $400,000 Was AI Compute Costs.

examples.body: $LULU I have NKE and buying ELF at around 51-52
